item.title: Openclaw Agents
item.shortDescription: Building AI agents that automate complex workflows and tasks.
item.description: Exploring the frontier of autonomous systems and practical AI applications through workflow automation agents.
item.whatItIs: A set of experiments with AI agents that can help run practical workflows.
item.whyIMadeIt: I want to understand where agents are genuinely useful and where they just add complexity.
item.whatILearned: Agents need tight scopes, visible feedback, and clear handoff points to stay useful.
item.nextStep: Document the best agent workflows and turn repeatable patterns into examples.
item.documentaryLead: This is where I test which agent workflows survive contact with real work. The interesting part is not that an agent can act. It is whether the loop stays visible, bounded, and useful.
item.currentQuestion: Where do agents save judgment work, and where do they just create a more confident mess?

proofArtifacts.label: Useful pattern
proofArtifacts.value: Agents work better when the task has a clear done state, visible logs, and a human review point.

proofArtifacts.label: Anti-pattern
proofArtifacts.value: Long autonomous loops with vague goals create impressive motion and weak accountability.

buildLog.title: Documenting the handoff points
buildLog.note: The best workflows are the ones where the agent knows when to stop and show its work.

item.summary: Building AI agents that automate complex workflows and tasks.
